slashCommands.name: deep-research
slashCommands.description: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. (dynamic workflow)

slashCommands.name: update-config
slashCommands.description: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.

slashCommands.name: verify
slashCommands.description: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.

slashCommands.name: debug
slashCommands.description: Enable debug logging for this session and help diagnose issues

slashCommands.name: code-review
slashCommands.description: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.

slashCommands.name: simplify
slashCommands.description: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.

slashCommands.name: batch
slashCommands.description: Research and plan a large-scale change, then execute it in parallel across 5–30 isolated worktree agents that each open a PR.

slashCommands.name: fewer-permission-prompts
slashCommands.description: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.

slashCommands.name: loop
slashCommands.description: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace.

slashCommands.name: schedule
slashCommands.description: Create, update, list, or run scheduled remote agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule.

slashCommands.name: claude-api
slashCommands.description:
Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).

slashCommands.name: run
slashCommands.description: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).

slashCommands.name: run-skill-generator
slashCommands.description: Author or improve the run-<unit> skill — a per-project skill that tells agents how to build, launch, and drive this project's app. Use when the user asks to set up the project, get it running, write run instructions, or verify build/run steps work from a clean environment.

slashCommands.name: clear
slashCommands.description: Start a new session with empty context; previous session stays on disk (resumable with /resume)

slashCommands.name: compact
slashCommands.description: Free up context by summarizing the conversation so far

slashCommands.name: context
slashCommands.description: Show current context usage

slashCommands.name: heapdump
slashCommands.description: Dump the JS heap to ~/Desktop

slashCommands.name: init
slashCommands.description: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation

slashCommands.name: reload-skills
slashCommands.description: Pick up skills added or changed on disk during this session

slashCommands.name: review
slashCommands.description: Review a pull request

slashCommands.name: security-review
slashCommands.description: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

slashCommands.name: usage
slashCommands.description: Show the total cost and duration of the current session

slashCommands.name: insights
slashCommands.description: Generate a report analyzing your Claude Code sessions

slashCommands.name: goal
slashCommands.description: Set a goal — keep working until the condition is met

slashCommands.name: team-onboarding
slashCommands.description: Help teammates ramp on Claude Code with a guide from your usage

versionAdvisory.message: Install the update now or review provider settings.
